title: Auto submit after filling a form
public_snippet_redacted: Open issue asks for an auto-submit option after Fake Filler fills a form and says the requester is happy to pay.
sprint_angle: Prepared browser-extension patch with disabled-by-default setting, settings UI, persisted option, and bounded form-submit behavior.
risk_boundary: No private browser data, credentials, payment data, production form targets, public posting, PR, direct message, or email without explicit approval.
acceptance_statement: I accept the Fake Filler Auto-Submit Sprint fixed-scope terms at $100. I understand work starts only after seller-owned external payment proof exists; I will provide only public or buyer-authorized non-sensitive requirements; the deliverable is limited to the prepared disabled-by-default auto-submit option, settings UI, saved option wiring, bounded submit behavior, apply notes, and verification notes; and production form testing, private browser/account data, store publication, public posting, pull requests, or ongoing revisions are not included unless separately agreed before payment.
response_draft_not_posted: I prepared a small local patch for the auto-submit request. It adds a disabled-by-default General Settings checkbox, persists an `autoSubmitAfterFill` option, and submits after `Fill this form`. For `Fill all inputs`, it submits only when the page has exactly one form, so it avoids guessing on multi-form pages. It uses `requestSubmit()` when available and falls back to a cancelable submit event plus `form.submit()`. I can hand over the patch or adapt it to a branch after fixed-scope acceptance and external payment proof.
posting_boundary: Draft response prepared only. External comments, emails, direct messages, pull requests, or patch delivery require explicit send approval.
focused_test: JSON parse; git diff whitespace check; full build blocked by upstream lockfile/dependency install state.
money_rule: Count $0 until real buyer acceptance, seller-owned external payment proof, delivery proof, and posted/released/payable/cleared funds exist.
